compilerFollowUps.title: Define consumer-runtime portable/metal benchmark corpus
compilerFollowUps.scope: generic haxe.rust benchmark planning; no downstream product names

architecture.qualityGoal: metal-like performance whenever haxe.rust can prove Haxe semantics are preserved
forbidden: performance shortcuts that change observable Haxe semantics

benchmarkCriteria.paritySignal: generated Rust shape, clone/temp count, cargo check/test
existing haxe.rust JSON benchmark plus semantic fixtures
portable/metal runtime ratio on hot state loops
typed host boundary shape and no raw app injection
metal generated behavior plus no-runtime lower-bound when applicable
typed boundary correctness before performance claims

decisionRule: Choose the codexhx source contract first.
Use metal for Rust-native host/runtime/tool behavior now.
Keep portable for Haxe-semantics DTO, codec, fixture, validation, config, and pure state surfaces.
Reduce portable-vs-metal performance gaps to generic haxe.rust fixtures or benchmarks.
Link future gaps from codexhx Beads to haxe.rust Beads.
